surface.name: Deepgram Make Your First API Request through pre-recorded transcription
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation home exposes a Fundamentals guide named Make Your First API Request. Its cURL route is the only option on that page that directly publishes a complete raw API request, while the Playground is a no-code trial and the SDK route redirects to product guides.

alternatives_considered.name: Deepgram Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Fundamentals guide presents this as a no-code, no-signup way to try the API rather than the authenticated developer API path.

alternatives_considered.name: Deepgram SDK route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The page requires language-specific environment setup and then redirects developers to a product Getting Started guide.

alternatives_considered.name: Streaming Speech-to-Text
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Speech-to-Text overview presents streaming and pre-recorded audio as use-case choices; the Fundamentals cURL example selects a hosted pre-recorded file.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Deepgram account and make the official cURL request until pre-recorded transcription data is returned.
assumed_prior_state: No Deepgram account, project, API key, billing method, request, or transcript is assumed.
A shell with cURL and network access is available; no signup, credential creation, credit use, or API request was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Not established end to end by public official documentation
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A no-account developer cannot be taken through the first authenticated transcription because the required account-registration transition is hidden inside the client-rendered Deepgram Console.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The public console response only states that JavaScript is required and does not expose signup fields, verification actions, or the completed-account state.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The account-registration transition required before the documented first API request is not publicly established.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official pre-recorded guide later demonstrates a JSON transcription response, but reaching its API-key prerequisite from no account would require inventing hidden console states.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Deepgram account before creating the first API key; the current signup transition is client-rendered and not publicly enumerated.
Create the first project API key in the Deepgram Console, assign its role and expiration settings, and copy its secret at the one-time display.
Have a shell with cURL for the published raw HTTP route.
The pay-as-you-go signup includes $200 credit and does not require a credit card for the first request.

primary_path.action: Open Make Your First API Request and follow its account prerequisite.
primary_path.output: The guide links to Deepgram account creation before API-key creation.
primary_path.success_signal: The official account prerequisite and first-request route are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide offers Playground, cURL, and SDK options; the cURL route is the direct authenticated request used for this surface.

primary_path.action: Open the Deepgram Console signup application.
primary_path.output: The public response is a JavaScript application shell.
primary_path.success_signal: No registration fields or completion transition are publicly inspectable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account input, identity-provider, verification, terms, and post-submit states are hidden.

candidate_path_gap.reason: After the hidden signup transition, Deepgram documents an automatically created first project, selecting that project, opening Settings and API Keys, choosing Create a New API Key, entering a friendly name, role, expiration, and optional tag, selecting Create Key, copying the one-time secret, and running the published cURL request against `/v1/listen` with the hosted audio URL. The pre-recorded guide shows a JSON response containing transcript, confidence, and word timing data after processing.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The post-account request is detailed, but it cannot become an end-to-end first-user candidate while mandatory signup states remain unobservable.

branches.path: Open Deepgram Playground, which the Fundamentals guide says can make a request without signup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids account and credential setup but changes the surface from an authenticated developer API request to a no-code trial.

branches.path: Configure the selected language environment, install its Deepgram SDK, and continue to a product-specific Getting Started guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds runtime and package setup and changes the execution route.

branches.condition: Transcribe a local file after account setup
branches.path: Send the audio bytes with the documented content type instead of submitting the hosted remote URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds local-file selection while preserving the transcription response terminal.

friction_gates.description: The mandatory signup application does not expose its form or transitions in the public official response.
The first API key must be created in the console, and its full secret is shown only once.
API-key creation requires choosing a role that controls the key's permitted actions.

sources.title: Make Your First API Request
sections_used: Make a Request to the API

sources.title: Creating API Keys
sections_used: Create an API key using the Deepgram Console

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, identity-provider choices, terms, verification actions, and completed-account state appear in the current Deepgram Console?
uncertainties.impact: The required account portion cannot be reconstructed or counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official guide links into a client-rendered application whose public response contains no signup states.

excluded_after_success: No complete first-success boundary is claimed while signup remains undocumented.
Streaming transcription, text-to-speech, Voice Agent, custom models, callbacks, production key rotation, and paid credit loading occur outside the documented first pre-recorded request.
